title: Google India UX Audit
subtitle: Democratizing Digital Access for Bharat's Next Billion Users
problem_statement: How might we optimize the Google search experience for Indian users who increasingly rely on vernacular voice commands and visual search while navigating varying network speeds?

notes.text: Users find switching keyboard languages cumbersome during search
Slow page loads on low-end smartphones create high bounce rates
Visual clutter in results distracts from primary information
Misinterpretation of regional accents in voice search
High demand for 'Search by Lens' for translating physical text
Integration of cricket and local festival updates as widgets
Education-focused search filters for government jobs (Sarkari)
Hyper-local discovery for UPI-enabled small businesses
Lack of intuitive 'offline mode' for saved search results
Limited support for hybrid language (Hinglish) queries
Inconsistent dark mode support across regional partner sites
Difficult navigation for first-time internet users (Bharat)
Autoplay video ads consuming limited daily data packs
Ambiguous 'Ad' labels that look like organic results
Difficulty in finding direct PDF downloads for govt forms
Cookie consent popups blocking the main search bar on mobile

personas.name: Arjun Sharma
personas.quote: I prefer speaking to my phone because typing in Hindi is too slow.
personas.bio: Arjun is preparing for civil service exams and relies heavily on mobile data. He often switches between English and Hindi depending on the complexity of the topic. He represents the aspirational youth in Tier-2 cities who use Google as their primary learning tool.
frustrations: Irrelevant global results for local queries
High data consumption on image-heavy pages

stages.actions: User realizes a need for information or a quick fact check.
Deciding between typing in English or using Voice Search in Hindi.
Selecting the most relevant query from the suggested dropdown.
Scanning snippets, Knowledge Panels, and People Also Ask sections.
Returning to Google for all information needs; trusting the ecosystem.

screens.title: Option B: Information Dashboard for Bharat

content.headline: Good Morning, Arjun
content.subheadline: Today's Cricket Score: IND vs AUS
